revisedPrompt:
Generate exactly one PNG image: a game-development pixel-art animated sprite sheet.

Use the attached reference image as the exact character identity reference. Treat this as re-posing the referenced character, not designing a new character. Match the reference character's proportions, head-to-body ratio, body width, palette, outline style, pixel density, sturdy grounded build, and overall scale exactly.

Canvas and layout:
- Exact image size: 1024x512 px.
- 2 rows x 4 columns, 8 frames total.
- Each frame cell is exactly 256x256 px.
- TOP row contains frames 1-4 left to right; BOTTOM row contains frames 5-8 left to right.
- Do not draw visible cell separator lines, grid lines, frame borders, frame numbers, labels, text, logos, UI, or watermarks.
- Background is solid #FF00FF chroma-key magenta filling all empty space, with no gradients, shadows, transparency, or texture.
- Exactly one character per cell, same character at the same scale in every frame, centered with clear solid magenta margin on all four sides.
- No body part, sword piece, clothing piece, tassel, pixel island, dust speck, seam sliver, or stray fragment may cross a cell border or float detached anywhere on the sheet.
- Feet must sit on the same ground line in every cell; head top must stay at the same height in every upright frame.

Style:
- Crisp high-resolution 16-bit-plus SNES-style pixel art, low-resolution pixel clusters, no anti-aliasing blur, no painterly smoothing.
- Consistent bright cheerful palette, one-pixel dark outline where useful, top-left light source.
- Bright mythical-Japan action RPG style.

Character: Mito, an androgynous 15-year-old wanderer, facing LEFT in a clean side profile / top-down 3/4 view walk cycle.
- Ink-black short hair tied into a tiny low ponytail at the nape.
- Vermilion braided-cord hair ornament.
- Calm amber eyes visible in left-facing side profile.
- White tube-sleeved top.
- Short indigo-blue haori jacket with small vermilion braided-cord knots on sleeves and hem.
- Loose young-leaf-green hakama trousers tucked into dark leggings with red cord ties.
- Light travel sandals.
- Vermilion braided cord belt-sash with a small tassel.
- Necklace with three pale-green magatama beads.
- At the left hip is a sheathed short ritual sword in a WHITE scabbard, wrapped with a thin sacred rope and tiny white paper streamers. It must read as a festival ritual implement, not a samurai katana.

Critical proportion lock:
- Match the attached reference character exactly: about 2.4 heads tall, sturdy grounded build, solid chest, thick limbs, broad stable stance.
- NOT slim, NOT lanky, NOT taller than the reference, NOT realistic adult proportions.
- The side view must keep the same head size, same total height, same sturdy body volume, same limb thickness, and same head-to-body ratio as the reference.
- Every frame depicts the same character identity; do not redraw, restyle, resize, or change facial/hair/clothing details between frames.

Sword rule:
- The white sheathed ritual sword stays attached at the hip in the SAME steady position, angle, length, and scale in EVERY frame.
- The scabbard faces the viewer in left profile.
- It must not swing loose, rotate, lengthen, shorten, drift forward/back, or be held in the hand.

Walk cycle, facing LEFT, 8 frames:
- Frame 1: CONTACT A, one foot planted forward toward the left, other foot trailing; opposite arm swings forward.
- Frame 2: weight drop, knees slightly bent, body slightly lower, planted foot flat and grounded.
- Frame 3: PASSING, feet together under the body, body 1-2 pixels higher.
- Frame 4: push-off, rear foot pushing away, arms and haori/tassels follow through.
- Frame 5: CONTACT B, the OTHER foot planted forward toward the left, mirror step of frame 1; opposite arm swings forward.
- Frame 6: weight drop for the opposite step.
- Frame 7: PASSING, feet together under the body, body 1-2 pixels higher.
- Frame 8: push-off for the opposite step.
- Legs must visibly alternate between stride frames; never repeat the same leading leg twice in a row.
- Arms swing opposite to legs and change pose together with the legs in every frame.
- Consecutive frames must have visibly different leg silhouettes. Do not copy the same leg pose between frames.
- Believable ground contact: planted foot flat on the ground directly under body weight, no floating, no tiptoeing, no sliding.
- Stride length must be consistent between left-foot and right-foot contact poses so the loop is even.
- Haori hem, braided cords, tassels, and paper streamers trail and sway subtly with the walk motion while staying attached.

Avoid completely:
- No Mario, Nintendo, Zelda, Final Fantasy, Pokemon, Dragon Quest, Secret of Mana, Okami, Sakuna characters, logos, or likeness.
- No samurai armor, no kabuto helmet, no ninja outfit, no two-sword daisho, no gun, no modern objects.
- No horror mood, no dark gloomy palette.
- No bird or companion creature.
- No letter emblems, modern text, watermark, or UI.

Output only the finished single PNG sprite sheet described above.
